title: zanthoxylum riedelianum subsp. mayanum
intro: Zanthoxylum riedelianum subsp. mayanum, also known as Mayan pepper or Mexican pepperleaf, is a plant species in the family Rutaceae, native to the tropical regions of Mexico and Central America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various health conditions. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its essential oils, which contain compounds such as limonene, pinene, and carvone, and have been shown to have health benefits including reducing pain, inflammation, and improving digestion. In herbal preparations, Mayan pepper is typically used as a dried leaf or root, and can be consumed as a tea, infused in oil, or used as a spice in cooking.
